iteration_scores.changes: Replaced verbose D3 skill with DeepSeek-style approach: static SVG for bar charts (no JavaScript), simplified D3 template for line charts with var/function syntax instead of const/arrow functions
Strengthened bar chart instructions with more explicit CRITICAL warnings, added 3-item example instead of 2, emphasized one rect per CSV row rule

Switched bar chart to Python gen_chart.py approach - model writes a Python script that generates static SVG HTML, avoiding JavaScript entirely. Changed title from 'D3.js Visualization' to 'Chart Visualization'
Restructured bar chart with numbered Action steps, moved IMPORTANT warning to end of bar chart section for emphasis
Changed bar chart to use inline python3 -c command instead of separate gen_chart.py file, simplified instructions

key_findings: qwen3-30b generates JavaScript with syntax errors (missing braces, broken template literals), causing D3-rendered SVG to fail
Static SVG approach (no JavaScript) for bar charts avoids syntax error problems entirely
Python script generation works when the model follows instructions, but qwen3-30b inconsistently ignores 'do not use JavaScript' directives
Line chart with pre-built HTML template containing <svg> and <path> elements plus simple var/function JS achieves 100% consistently
Using var instead of const and function() instead of arrow functions reduces syntax errors in the model's JS output
Model sometimes tries to reimplement D3.js from memory instead of downloading it, producing broken code
